thesis: platform manages company; company manages business; business is mainly carried by project in construction execution

family_hierarchy.hierarchy_statement: The platform defines company, capability, pack, and approval governance; it does not use project as the management root.
The company manages counterparties as a business network; projects may scope usage but do not own identity.
Business execution is mainly carried by project; project is the construction execution carrier, not the platform root.
The company manages product and material identity; project uses those identities during expense execution.
The company manages income opportunities and commitments; tender may precede project while income contracts usually bind to project execution.
The company manages expense commitments; construction expense is normally carried by project, with general contracts as declared exceptions.
The company manages payment intent and execution as expense facts; project is the normal cost and approval carrier.

family_hierarchy.family: receipt income invoice and tax realization
family_hierarchy.hierarchy_statement: The company manages income realization and tax evidence; project is the normal carrier for construction income facts.
The company manages treasury accounts and reconciliation; project may scope cash movement but account master can remain company-level.

family_hierarchy.family: project budget BOQ and cost control
family_hierarchy.hierarchy_statement: Business cost control is carried by project through budget, BOQ, cost period, and ledger.
Material procurement, acceptance, movement, rental, and settlement are expense execution facts normally carried by project.
Labor, equipment, and subcontract usage/settlement are expense execution facts normally carried by project.

family_hierarchy.family: progress quality safety risk and diary
family_hierarchy.hierarchy_statement: Progress, quality, safety, risk, and diary facts describe project execution control.

family_hierarchy.family: document admin payroll and office operations
family_hierarchy.hierarchy_statement: Administrative and payroll evidence is company-managed; project may scope the document when it belongs to execution.
The company consumes visibility over business and projects; projections derive from facts and do not own the carrier.
Source systems provide historical evidence; replay may map to company/business/project but must not define the hierarchy.

family_hierarchy.family: workflow approval dictionary audit and validation
family_hierarchy.hierarchy_statement: The platform governs approval, dictionary, validation, and audit policies used by company-managed business.

family_hierarchy.family: scene capability subscription and frontend contract runtime
family_hierarchy.hierarchy_statement: The platform governs capability, scene, subscription, and frontend runtime contracts; project is only one possible entry context.
Compatibility bridges keep native/OCA roots usable for company-managed business without changing the hierarchy.
